findings.issue: LSIcon defines a new app-local IconName enum instead of consuming the generated design-owned catalog enum from LaneShadowTheme. The generated catalog already exists at tokens/platforms/swift/Sources/LaneShadowTheme/Generated/Tokens.swift:207 as Tokens.icon.name.
findings.impact: This breaks the core scope of UC-ATM-10: downstream callers are typed against a parallel enum, not the generated catalog owned by UC-TOK-05.

findings.issue: The renderer hand-authors all glyphs as Swift Path/Canvas code instead of rendering assets from the generated design-owned SVG catalog.
findings.impact: Violates the task constraints: catalog comes from UC-TOK-05, missing icons must be added upstream, and LSIcon must render typed glyphs from the design-owned SVG catalog. There is no generic/hash fallback or SF Symbol fallback in the changed file, but it is still not using the catalog.

findings.issue: LaneShadowStories.all appends LSIconStories.all, but this file also declares a private placeholder LSIconStories at line 65. That local placeholder returns Text("LSIcon catalog") and Text("LSIcon color overrides") at lines 74 and 84, so the real stories in ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/LSIconStories.swift are not what the aggregator registers.
findings.impact: AC-6 is not satisfied: the registered stories have the required ids but do not render the catalog or color override story implementations.

findings.issue: The new Swift files are not present in the explicit Xcode source build phases. rg found no references to LSIcon.swift, IconSize.swift, LSIconStories.swift, LSIconTests.swift, or LSIconTypeSafetyTests.swift in the project file; the test sources list at lines 486-497 and app sources list at lines 500-526 include only existing files.
findings.impact: The implementation and tests are not compiled by the Xcode project as currently committed. The no-.xcodeproj diff constraint is satisfied, but the task cannot be validated as an integrated iOS implementation without target membership being handled.

findings.issue: The public initializer uses color: IconContentColor = .primary rather than the task-specified typed content color API color: ContentColor = .primary.
findings.impact: Raw Color is not exposed, but the public API does not match the required ContentColor contract.

findings.issue: The raw Color rejection check is only a commented-out line, not an executable compile-fail gate. LSIconTests.swift also makes catalog coverage tautological through hasDrawable(for:) returning IconName.allCases.contains(name).
findings.impact: AC-4 and catalog basics are not meaningfully covered, even before considering that the new test files are not in the Xcode project.

validation.scope_check: pass: committed diff contains only SCOPE.writeAllowed files and no .kb-run*, .xcodeproj, token, Android, native-theme, or native-sandbox files
validation.changed_file_sf_symbol_grep: pass: no genericCatalogPath, Image(systemName:), UIImage(systemName:), or systemName: matches in changed files
validation.project_wide_sf_symbol_grep: fail/residual: existing out-of-scope SF Symbol references remain across ios/LaneShadow; changed files did not add them
validation.story_id_grep: partial: atoms.icon.catalog, atoms.icon.colorOverrides, and LSIconStories.all are present, but aggregator resolves to the private placeholder stories in LaneShadowStories.swift
validation.literal_stroke_size_color_grep: pass: no lineWidth: 1.5, numeric frame(width: ...), or Color.red/green/etc literals in LSIcon.swift
validation.pnpm_icons_check: not_validated: failed because node_modules is missing and tsx was not found
validation.git_status: dirty: .kb-run-sprint-codex/.state.json.sha256 is modified outside the implementation commit
validation.git_diff_check: pass with macOS xcrun cache warnings caused by read-only temp cache restrictions

residual_risk: Full xcodebuild, XCTest, and swiftformat gates were not run in this read-only review; the Xcode project currently does not include the new implementation/test files anyway.
The broader ios/LaneShadow SF Symbol grep still fails on pre-existing files; per instruction this is reported as residual/out-of-scope rather than an implementation defect.
pnpm icons:check could not run because dependencies are not installed in this worktree.
